text: Hello, this is a new video for a new case of the difficult condition that we treated during the week of the stroke. We decided to perform a surgery to remove these ligaments and to contract the case in a surgical manner. The first step was to enter the abdominal cavity through what I call the wang point, which is a point located in the middle between the back of the abdomen and the beginning of the chest cavity. As you saw in the beginning of the video, there was a curtain of shells surrounding the intestines that blocked the vision from the uterus area. After dealing with it, we found these precise intestines covered inside the stomach wall. Since the patient does not suffer from previous symptoms, due to the presence of these types of splinters, so we decided to cooperate and consult with the surgeon who was present, the surgeon of the digestive system who was present for consultation, that there is no need for these fine marks.

The surgeon uses scissors to carefully dissect and dismantle adhesions connecting a mass to the side wall of the pelvic basin. The yellow tissue is lowered, revealing the straight line of the mass, as the dissection continues to clarify the boundaries of the masses.

The surgeon carefully navigates the strong adhesions in the region, using a protective screen to avoid damaging the intestines while attempting to remove the rectum from the surrounding tissue. The boundaries of the tissue bag are now clear, and the surgeon begins to collect the bag without further deep dissection to minimize damage.

The surgeon completes the extraction of a mass and checks for any residual liquid using a screen. A plastic bag is placed to contain the mass, ensuring no contact with the uterine wall during the procedure. A Surgicil mesh is inserted to prevent chemical interactions at the surgical site, followed by the placement of a drain tube to manage a blood clot.
The operation ended safely and the patient went to the hospital on the second day and was in good condition. Thank you for watching.
